title: Requirements Traceability Lab
goal: Turn requirements, interfaces, tests, and risks into structured engineering data with automated validation and exportable reports.

phases.name: Define the case study
steps: Choose a realistic mission with multiple subsystems and visible system interactions.
Write stakeholder-level needs and derive engineering requirements.
Allocate subsystem responsibilities and define a stable ID scheme.

phases.name: Structure the engineering data
steps: Store stakeholder, system, and subsystem requirements in machine-readable files.
Add components, interfaces, verification cases, and a risk register.
Make parent-child traces explicit so audits can be automated.

phases.name: Build the automation
steps: Implement artifact loaders and an audit engine.
Validate traceability, verification linkage, interface consistency, and risk data.
Export reviewer-facing Markdown and CSV reports.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Add regression tests for the clean dataset.
Inject negative cases such as broken interfaces and missing links.
Fix validation logic until the repository audits cleanly.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write an English README, add licensing, and organize the docs folder.
Commit generated reports so reviewers can inspect outputs immediately.
Push the repo publicly and keep CI green.

todos.task: Select a mission scenario with at least five subsystems
Model stakeholder, system, and subsystem requirements
Define components, interfaces, verification tests, and risks
Implement the audit CLI and report exporter
Add unit tests and negative validation tests
Generate audit reports into the reports folder
Publish and maintain the public repository

title: Satellite Power Budget Simulator
goal: Simulate solar generation, eclipse losses, battery usage, and subsystem loads across orbital mission phases.

phases.name: Define the mission assumptions
steps: Select a cubesat or microsat mission concept and target orbit.
List subsystem load cases for sunlight, eclipse, safe mode, and payload mode.
Document battery, solar array, and depth-of-discharge assumptions.

phases.name: Build the data model
steps: Create machine-readable scenario inputs for orbital periods and subsystem duty cycles.
Define reusable configuration files for power generation and consumption.
Capture nominal and worst-case scenarios.

phases.name: Implement the simulator
steps: Calculate orbital energy balance across mission segments.
Simulate state-of-charge evolution over multiple orbits.
Export plots and CSV tables for each mission scenario.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Cross-check sample scenarios against hand calculations.
Test edge cases such as prolonged eclipse or payload overuse.
Refine model assumptions when margins look unrealistic.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write a clear assumptions section and scenario guide.
Include comparison charts and an example mission run.
Push the repo with generated figures and validation notes.

todos.task: Choose a reference satellite mission and orbit
Create sunlight and eclipse load-case assumptions
Model solar array and battery parameters
Add plots for state of charge and power margins
Validate the model against hand calculations
Write a technical README and scenario guide
Publish example results and push the repo

title: Interface Control Toolkit
goal: Model subsystem interfaces, validate producer-consumer compatibility, and export interface control documentation.

phases.name: Define the integration context
steps: Pick a system with at least four interacting subsystems.
List critical interfaces, protocols, data items, and timing constraints.
Set naming rules for signals, interface IDs, and ownership.

phases.name: Build the interface schema
steps: Store components and their provided and required signals in machine-readable files.
Model each interface with source, target, protocol, payload, units, and update rate.
Capture versioning or compatibility metadata.

phases.name: Implement the toolkit
steps: Validate that every source produces the declared signal.
Validate that every target consumes the declared signal.
Export an interface register and an ICD-style summary document.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Create broken sample interfaces to prove the validator catches errors.
Test naming mismatches, missing units, and duplicate identifiers.
Fix schema gaps that make reviews harder.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Add diagrams or tables that explain the integration context.
Document how a reviewer adds a new interface.
Publish example exports and push the repo.

todos.task: Choose the reference system and subsystem set
Define an interface schema with timing and units
Generate interface register and ICD exports
Add negative tests for mismatched interfaces
Write onboarding documentation for new interfaces
Create a sample dataset and diagrams

title: Verification Readiness Dashboard
goal: Track requirement verification status, evidence, owners, and closure risk in a reviewer-friendly dashboard.

phases.name: Define the verification model
steps: Define requirement, verification case, owner, due date, and evidence fields.
Decide which status states matter, such as planned, in-work, blocked, and closed.
Choose metrics such as closure percentage and open critical items.

phases.name: Build the source datasets
steps: Create machine-readable requirement and verification records.
Link evidence files or references to each verification case.
Capture subsystem ownership and review status.

phases.name: Implement the dashboard
steps: Compute readiness metrics by subsystem and verification method.
Render a static HTML dashboard or exportable summary tables.
Highlight blocked or overdue verification items.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Test missing evidence, duplicated cases, and inconsistent statuses.
Verify that dashboard totals match the source data.
Refine the display so a reviewer can understand status quickly.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Add screenshots and a sample readiness review pack.
Document how to add new requirements and evidence.
Push the repo and optionally deploy GitHub Pages.

todos.task: Design the verification status data model
Implement subsystem and method readiness metrics
Render a static dashboard or HTML report
Add tests for missing or inconsistent evidence
Document update workflow for new verification cases

deliverables: Public repository or GitHub Pages deployment

title: Trade Study Engine
goal: Compare architecture options against weighted decision criteria and make trade study logic reproducible.

phases.name: Define the decision problem
steps: Choose an architecture decision such as propulsion, communications, or sensing options.
Define the candidate concepts and evaluation criteria.
Assign rationale and initial weights for each criterion.

phases.name: Build the scoring data
steps: Create machine-readable alternatives, scores, assumptions, and references.
Separate raw technical data from weighted decision logic.
Capture uncertainty bounds or confidence notes.

phases.name: Implement the engine
steps: Calculate weighted scores and rank alternatives.
Generate tables and charts that explain why the preferred option wins.
Support quick what-if analysis by changing weights.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Test that weights sum correctly and rankings update as expected.
Validate edge cases such as tied scores or missing inputs.
Check that published conclusions match the underlying math.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write a decision narrative around the trade study.
Include sensitivity results and assumptions in the README.
Push the repo with example scenarios and outputs.

todos.task: Choose a concrete architecture decision to analyze
Store scoring inputs in structured files
Implement weighted ranking and what-if analysis
Test score normalization and ranking changes

title: FMEA and Reliability Workbench
goal: Connect failure modes, causes, effects, controls, and residual risk into a structured engineering analysis workspace.

phases.name: Define the reliability scope
steps: Pick a subsystem or mission concept with meaningful failure modes.
Define severity, occurrence, and detection scales.
Choose how mitigations and controls will be recorded.

phases.name: Build the analysis dataset
steps: Create structured records for functions, failure modes, causes, and effects.
Link mitigations, controls, and residual risks to each failure mode.
Add subsystem ownership and review status.

phases.name: Implement the workbench
steps: Calculate risk priority values and highlight the highest-risk items.
Generate sortable tables or Markdown reports for review boards.
Trace mitigations back to affected functions or requirements.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Test invalid scale values, duplicate failure modes, and missing mitigations.
Verify that risk priority calculations match the documented method.
Review whether mitigations actually reduce the residual score.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Document the scoring model and review assumptions clearly.
Include a sample FMEA review report in the repository.
Push the repo with generated outputs and test coverage.

todos.task: Select the system or subsystem to analyze
Define severity, occurrence, and detection scales
Model failure modes, causes, effects, and controls
Trace mitigations to functions or requirements
Add tests for invalid scores and missing controls
Write a review-ready README and sample report

title: Autonomous Disaster Response Digital Thread
goal: Tie multiple systems engineering repositories into one program-level digital thread with subrepositories, generated portfolio artifacts, and recruiter-friendly program navigation.

phases.name: Select the umbrella theme
steps: Compare several large-scale systems engineering program concepts.
Choose a theme that can justify multiple lifecycle workstreams under one narrative.
Document why the selected direction reads as a coherent system-of-systems.

phases.name: Define the program structure
steps: Map each child repository to a lifecycle phase or systems engineering concern.
Create a machine-readable manifest with repo names, local paths, lifecycle roles, and URLs.
Write recruiter guidance and system-of-systems framing documents.

phases.name: Build the umbrella automation
steps: Implement a validator for workstream metadata, submodule registration, and repository health.
Export program-level Markdown, CSV, and HTML artifacts for reviewers.
Add tests that keep the manifest and submodule inventory aligned.

phases.name: Link and verify subrepositories
steps: Add child repositories under `workstreams/` as Git submodules.
Run end-to-end tests and fix any path, metadata, or export issues.
Regenerate program reports so the umbrella repo is review-ready on first open.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write a top-level README that explains the portfolio logic clearly.
License the umbrella repo and configure CI with recursive submodule checkout.
Create the public GitHub repo, push it, and add descriptive topics.

todos.task: Evaluate multiple large-scale umbrella repo themes
Create the workstream manifest and recruiter docs
Implement the umbrella validator and exporter CLI
Add all child repositories as submodules
Run validation, generate program reports, and fix issues
Publish and push the umbrella repository

deliverables: Generated HTML and Markdown program dashboard

title: Program Risk Board
goal: Aggregate multi-repository technical and integration risks into a program-level review board with structured scoring and exportable decision artifacts.

phases.name: Define the board model
steps: Choose the program-level risk fields, owners, and review gates.
Define a repeatable scoring method for initial and residual exposure.
Decide how workstream references and mitigation actions will be captured.

phases.name: Build the risk dataset
steps: Create machine-readable program risks linked to specific repositories.
Capture review gate ownership and mitigation plans.
Add enough variety to show open, mitigating, and watch states.

phases.name: Implement the board automation
steps: Validate scoring ranges and required fields.
Calculate initial and residual RPN values.
Export summary, register, and gate-level rollups.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Add tests for invalid scores and broken records.
Check that critical residual risks are flagged correctly.
Fix report formatting until the repo is reviewer-ready.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write the README and process notes clearly.
Commit generated artifacts for immediate inspection.
Push the public repository and add metadata.

todos.task: Define the risk schema and scoring model
Create a realistic program-level risk dataset
Generate board outputs into the reports folder

title: Ops Concept Simulator
goal: Model operational mission threads, handoffs, and subsystem participation across disaster-response scenarios in a structured ConOps repository.

phases.name: Define the mission threads
steps: Choose realistic disaster-response scenarios.
Break each scenario into ordered operational phases.
List which systems participate in each phase.

phases.name: Build the scenario dataset
steps: Store phase durations and active systems in machine-readable form.
Capture mission objectives and scenario-level structure.
Make handoffs explicit through ordered phase data.

phases.name: Implement the simulator
steps: Validate scenario completeness and phase durations.
Calculate total scenario time and subsystem utilization.
Export scenario and utilization reports.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Add tests for invalid durations and broken phase definitions.
Check that total hours and handoffs match the source data.
Fix warnings so the default dataset simulates cleanly.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write ConOps notes and a clear README.
Commit generated dashboards and tables.
Push publicly and add GitHub metadata.

todos.task: Eliminate warnings in the default dataset

title: Autonomous Disaster Response Program Office
goal: Create a true program-office scale umbrella repository that links direct hubs and reports nested workstreams through a layered submodule topology.

phases.name: Select the mega-repo theme
steps: Compare several large-scale umbrella concepts.
Select a theme that supports direct hubs and nested workstreams.
Document the selection rationale.

phases.name: Build the direct hub layer
steps: Reuse the digital-thread hub.
Create governance and operations hubs as independent repositories.
Define the office-level manifest that ties them together.

phases.name: Implement office automation
steps: Validate submodule registration and local hub health.
Count nested workstream declarations from the digital-thread hub.
Export recruiter-facing program office artifacts.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Add tests for missing fields and broken topology declarations.
Run the office validator against real local submodules.
Fix path and reporting issues until the office repo audits cleanly.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write architecture, recruiter, and plan documents.
Configure recursive-submodule CI.
Push the public umbrella repo and add GitHub topics.

todos.task: Create the direct governance and operations hubs
Build the office-level validator and exporter
Add all direct hubs as submodules

title: MBSE Model Registry
goal: Catalog model packages, architecture views, and repository allocations so the portfolio shows an explicit MBSE and architecture-governance layer.

phases.name: Define the modeling scope
steps: Choose which architecture domains need model packages.
Decide which view types should be represented.
Map packages to the repositories they inform.

phases.name: Build the package dataset
steps: Store package IDs, domains, and view types in machine-readable form.
Link each package to related repositories.
Capture requirement and interface coverage counts.

phases.name: Implement the registry
steps: Validate package structure and required fields.
Roll up repository coverage across model packages.
Export a catalog and dashboard for reviewers.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Add tests for empty view lists and malformed records.
Check that coverage counts match the source data.
Fix any exporter gaps.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write MBSE-oriented documentation.
Commit generated artifacts for reviewers.
Push the public repository and add GitHub topics.

todos.task: Link the ecosystem repo into model coverage

title: Release Readiness Command Center
goal: Track which portfolio repositories are actually publishable by capturing release gates, blockers, CI state, documentation readiness, and demo readiness.

phases.name: Define readiness criteria
steps: Choose which publication gates matter.
Define what counts as ready, needs work, or blocked.
Decide how blockers will be stored.

phases.name: Build the readiness dataset
steps: Create one record per repository.
Capture CI, docs, demo, license, and automation states.
Record blockers explicitly for non-ready repos.

phases.name: Implement the command center
steps: Validate allowed statuses.
Roll up readiness counts across repositories.
Export repo-level and blocker-level artifacts.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Add tests for invalid CI states and malformed blockers.
Check summary counts against the source data.
Fix reporting gaps until the repo builds cleanly.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write clear governance-focused docs.
Commit generated outputs for inspection.
Push publicly and add GitHub topics.

todos.task: Build a realistic portfolio readiness dataset
Mark the ecosystem release as ready after publication

title: Federated MBSE Ecosystem
goal: Create the largest umbrella in the portfolio by combining a program-office hub, an MBSE model registry hub, and a release-governance hub into one layered ecosystem.

phases.name: Select the ecosystem concept
steps: Compare enterprise-scale umbrella ideas.
Choose one that supports multiple direct hubs and nested descendants.
Document the selection logic.

phases.name: Build the hub layer
steps: Reuse the program-office hub.
Create MBSE and release-governance hubs as separate repos.
Define the ecosystem manifest.

phases.name: Implement ecosystem automation
steps: Validate direct hub registration.
Count descendants recursively through nested `.gitmodules` files.
Export ecosystem-level summary artifacts.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Add tests for missing fields and topology mismatches.
Initialize nested submodules recursively.
Fix validation gaps until the ecosystem builds cleanly.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write architecture and recruiter guidance docs.
Commit generated outputs and topology summaries.
Push publicly and add GitHub topics.

todos.task: Build the ecosystem validator and exporter
Add all direct hubs as submodules
Initialize and validate nested descendants recursively

title: Portfolio Analytics Observatory
goal: Measure cross-repository portfolio health through structured metrics covering automation, documentation, integration depth, and layer-level maturity.

phases.name: Define observability metrics
steps: Choose scores and states that matter across repositories.
Define portfolio layers and readiness categories.
Set acceptable score ranges.

phases.name: Build the metrics dataset
steps: Create one metric record per repository.
Capture automation, documentation, and integration values.
Assign each repo to a portfolio layer.

phases.name: Implement the observatory
steps: Validate score ranges and statuses.
Roll up metrics by layer.
Export reviewer-facing scorecards and dashboards.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Add tests for invalid scores and malformed records.
Check layer averages against the source data.
Fix report formatting and data-quality warnings.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write observability-focused docs.
Commit generated outputs.
Push publicly and add GitHub topics.

todos.task: Mark the enterprise platform as ready after publication

title: Supplier Interface Exchange
goal: Track supplier-facing interfaces, delivery gates, and required exchange documents so the portfolio shows external integration and delivery governance.

phases.name: Define supplier exchange scope
steps: Choose which external interfaces matter.
Define lifecycle states and delivery gates.
Decide which documents must be tracked.

phases.name: Build the source dataset
steps: Create one record per supplier-facing interface.
Capture owning repo, protocol, and delivery gate.
Record required supporting documents.

phases.name: Implement the exchange audit
steps: Validate statuses and required fields.
Roll up coverage by delivery gate.
Export supplier and gate-level reports.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Add tests for invalid statuses and malformed document lists.
Check gate counts against the source data.
Fix warnings so the default dataset audits cleanly.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write supplier-facing docs.
Commit generated outputs.
Push publicly and add GitHub topics.

todos.task: Eliminate warnings in the default dataset

title: Autonomous Disaster Response Enterprise Platform
goal: Create the top-most enterprise umbrella in the portfolio by combining the federated ecosystem, enterprise analytics, and supplier governance hubs into one layered platform.

phases.name: Select the enterprise concept
steps: Evaluate enterprise-scale umbrella ideas.
Choose one that extends the ecosystem layer with new direct hubs.
Document the selection rationale.

phases.name: Build the enterprise hub layer
steps: Reuse the federated ecosystem hub.
Create analytics and supplier-governance hubs.
Define the enterprise manifest.

phases.name: Implement enterprise automation
steps: Validate direct hub registration.
Count descendants recursively through nested `.gitmodules` files.
Export enterprise-level topology artifacts.

phases.name: Debug and verify
steps: Add tests for missing fields and topology mismatches.
Initialize nested submodules recursively.
Fix any reporting or path gaps.

phases.name: Publish professionally
steps: Write enterprise architecture and recruiter docs.
Commit generated outputs and dashboards.
Push publicly and add GitHub topics.

todos.task: Build the enterprise validator and exporter
Add all direct hubs as submodules
Initialize and validate nested descendants recursively
